issues.name: post-charge-crash-safety
issues.description: Ensure process never crashes after charging a card - user must always get success response

context.scenario: User checkout → NMI.RunSale() succeeds (card charged) → subsequent step fails/panics → user sees error
context.problem: If we charge a card and then crash before returning success, the user sees an error but their card was charged. This is catastrophic UX and could cause support issues, chargebacks, or lost trust.
context.critical_window: Between NMI.RunSale() returning success and HTTP 200 being sent to user

requirements: After a successful charge, ALL subsequent operations must be wrapped in recover() or equivalent
If RegisterPurchase() fails after charge, log the error but still return success to user
Payment record MUST be created even if entitlement granting fails
Consider: persist payment record BEFORE granting entitlements (two-phase)
Consider: background job to reconcile failed entitlement grants
Never let a panic propagate after card is charged
affected_code: Any code path that charges a card synchronously
audit_steps: Review processNMISale() for panic safety after RunSale()
Ensure Payment record is created before entitlements
Test: what happens if DB connection drops right after charge?
Test: what happens if entitlement service panics?

issues.name: nmi-test-account-integration
issues.description: Set up real Mobius/NMI test account for integration tests that hit the NMI API
steps: CURRENT: Tests use public NMI demo key which rejects operations on non-existent subscriptions
TARGET: Use real Mobius test account so we can create/cancel real test subscriptions
Obtain test credentials from Mobius (security_key, api_key)
Update testcontainer_suite.go NMI config with Mobius test credentials
Tests can then create real subscriptions via webhook simulation and cancel them via API
BENEFIT: Full integration testing of NMI flows with real data
tests_blocked: TestCancelSubscriptionNMI - tries to cancel non-existent subscription
TestCancelSubscriptionEmptyFeedback - tries to cancel non-existent subscription
TestSubscribeNMISuccess - requires valid NMI subscription creation

issues.description: Allow users to change which stored payment method their NMI subscription uses

[DONE]   - Validates user owns the subscription (from JWT)
[DONE]   - Validates user owns the target payment method
[DONE]   - Validates payment method is active and NMI-backed

[DONE]   - Validates subscription is active or past_due (not cancelled)

issues.name: unified-checkout-one-time-purchases
issues.description: Unified checkout endpoint that handles both subscriptions and one-time purchases based on Price.BillingCycleDays
steps: [DONE] NMI Client:
[DONE]   - Add RunSale(vaultID, amount int64, currency, description string) to internal/integrations/nmi/nmi.go
[DONE]   - NMI API: type=sale, customer_vault_id=X, amount=Y, order_description=Z

[DONE]   - Update AddRecurringSubscription to accept optional start_date parameter for delayed-start subscriptions

[DONE]   - Create POST /v1/me/checkout endpoint
[DONE]   - REMOVE old endpoints: POST /v1/subscriptions/:processor (mobius, ccbill, solana) - replaced by unified checkout
[DONE]   - Request: { price_id, payment_method_id } OR { price_id, payment_token }
[DONE]   - Look up Price and check BillingCycleDays:
[DONE]   - If BillingCycleDays != nil → subscription flow (see deduplication below)
[DONE]   - If BillingCycleDays == nil → one-time purchase flow
[DONE] Deduplication Logic (CRITICAL - check BOTH subscriptions AND entitlements):
[DONE]   - Get user's active entitlements for the Product (from any source: subscription, one-off, admin)
[DONE]   - Get user's active subscriptions for the Product

[DONE]   - If coverage has an end date → Allow purchase with delayed start = coverage_end_date
[DONE]   - If no active coverage → Allow immediate purchase
[DONE]   - NOTE: No 'expiring soon' threshold - user can buy anytime if coverage has an end date.
[DONE] Processor-Specific Handling for Delayed Start:
[DONE]   - CCBill: BLOCK if user has existing coverage - CCBill API cannot schedule future start dates
[DONE]   - NMI subscription: Create with start_date = coverage_end_date, status=pending, charged on start_date
[DONE]   - Solana/one-off: Charge immediately, but create entitlement with start_at = coverage_end_date
[DONE] NMI Subscription Flow:
[DONE]   - If immediate: create NMI subscription with no start_date (charges now)
[DONE]   - If delayed: create NMI subscription with start_date = coverage_end_date
[DONE]   - Store locally with status=pending until webhook confirms first charge

[DONE]   - Create Payment record with purchased_at = now
[DONE]   - If delayed start needed: Create entitlement with start_at = coverage_end_date
[DONE]   - If immediate start: Create entitlement with start_at = now

[DONE]   - Product.EntitlementsSpec has { entitlement_name: duration_days }

[DONE]   - User has NMI sub expiring → buys Solana one-off → Solana entitlement starts when NMI ends
[DONE]   - User has Solana entitlement expiring → creates NMI sub → NMI sub starts when Solana ends
[DONE]   - User has CCBill sub expiring → buys NMI sub → NMI starts when CCBill ends
[DONE]   - User has any coverage → tries CCBill → BLOCKED (CCBill limitation)

issues.name: cli-duplicate-detection
issues.description: CLI command to detect duplicate charges and multiple active subscriptions for the same user
steps: Create cmd/billing-cli/main.go with cobra CLI framework
Add 'duplicates' command with subcommands:
- 'billing-cli duplicates charges' - find users charged twice in same billing period
- Query payments grouped by user_id, price_id, month
- Flag any user with >1 successful payment for same price in same 30-day window
- Output: user_id, payment_ids, amounts, dates, recommendation (refund which one)
- 'billing-cli duplicates subscriptions' - find users with multiple active subscriptions
- Query subscriptions WHERE status='active' GROUP BY user_id HAVING count > 1
- Output: user_id, subscription_ids, prices, start_dates, recommendation (cancel which one)
Add --format flag for output (table, json, csv)
Add --dry-run flag (default) vs --fix flag for auto-remediation (with confirmation prompt)
Add date range filters (--since, --until) to limit search scope
Write tests for duplicate detection logic

issues.name: cli-orphan-entitlements
issues.description: CLI command to detect users with active entitlements that should have expired
steps: Add 'orphans' command to billing-cli:
- 'billing-cli orphans entitlements' - find users with premium but no valid source
Query logic:
- Get all users with active entitlements (end_at IS NULL OR end_at > now, revoked_at IS NULL)
- For each, check if they have a valid source:
- source_type='subscription' → verify subscription is still active and not expired
- source_type='one_off' → verify payment exists and end_at hasn't passed
- source_type='admin' → SKIP (intentionally granted, don't flag)
- Flag entitlements where source is invalid/expired but entitlement still active
Output columns:
- user_id, entitlement_id, entitlement_name, source_type, source_id
- source_status (e.g., 'subscription_cancelled', 'subscription_expired', 'payment_refunded')
- entitlement_start, expected_end, recommendation
Add --include-admin flag to also audit admin-granted entitlements (off by default)
Add --format flag (table, json, csv)
Add --fix flag to auto-revoke orphaned entitlements (with confirmation prompt)
Write tests for orphan detection logic

issues.name: admin-user-search
issues.description: Sophisticated user search for admin dashboard
steps: Design search API:
- GET /v1/admin/users?q=...&filters... - search users with billing data
Search fields:
- email (partial match from subscriptions/payments)
- user_id (exact match)
- processor_subscription_id (exact match for support lookups)
- transaction_id (exact match for payment support)
Filters:
- has_subscription=true/false
- subscription_status=active/cancelled/past_due/etc
- processor=nmi/ccbill/solana
- has_entitlement=premium/etc
- created_after, created_before (date range)
Sorting:
- sort_by=newest/oldest/last_payment/subscription_start
Pagination:
- page, page_size, cursor-based pagination for large results
Performance:
- Add database indexes for search fields
- Consider full-text search for email
- Rate limit search queries
NOTE: Billing service searches its own data. Full user profile search (username, etc) lives in main doujins API.

issues.name: admin-rate-limiting
issues.description: Add rate limiting to admin endpoints to limit blast radius of compromised JWT
steps: PROBLEM: If admin JWT is leaked, attacker could cancel/refund thousands of users before detection
Implement per-admin-user rate limiting using Redis (keyed by admin user ID from JWT)
Define rate limits for destructive operations: e.g., 5 cancellations/minute, 10/hour, 50/day
Define rate limits for bulk operations: e.g., 3 extend operations/minute
On rate limit exceeded: lock out admin for extended period (e.g., 1 hour) and alert
Add alerting/notification when rate limits are approached (e.g., 80% threshold)
Create admin rate limit middleware that wraps destructive endpoints
Allow super-admin or manual override to unlock rate-limited admin if legitimate
Log all rate limit events for security audit
BENEFIT: Limits blast radius - attacker can only affect ~5-10 users before getting locked out

issues.name: solana-devnet-integration-tests
issues.description: Add real Solana devnet integration tests that move test SOL/tokens on-chain
steps: SETUP COMPLEXITY: Medium-high. Requires funded devnet wallet and handling rate limits/faucet availability.
Create test wallet keypair specifically for integration tests (store in config or env)
Fund test wallet via Solana devnet faucet (solana airdrop) - may need retry logic due to rate limits
Create test for full payment flow: generate intent -> sign transaction -> submit -> verify on-chain
Use devnet RPC endpoint (https://api.devnet.solana.com or Helius/QuickNode devnet)
Test SOL transfer: create intent for X lamports, sign with test wallet, submit, verify balance changes
Test SPL token transfer: create test SPL token on devnet, test token payment flow
Handle devnet instability: add retries, longer timeouts, skip tests if devnet unavailable
Consider using localnet (solana-test-validator) for faster/more reliable tests as alternative
Add -tags=devnet build tag to separate from unit tests (expensive/slow to run)
Document devnet wallet setup and faucet requirements in README

issues.name: remove-wallet-verification-requirement
issues.description: Remove mandatory wallet verification before Solana payments - it's unnecessary since transaction signatures already prove ownership
steps: CURRENT: User must link+verify wallet via challenge/sign before making a payment
PROBLEM: This is an unnecessary step - the transaction signature itself proves wallet ownership
Update CreatePaymentIntent to accept any wallet address (not just verified ones)
Update ConfirmPaymentIntent to verify the signed transaction's fee payer matches the claimed wallet
Keep wallet linking endpoints for optional UX convenience (remember wallets) but don't require verification for payments
Update GeneratePayment handler similarly
Remove isVerified checks from payment flows
Update tests to reflect new behavior (payments work without pre-verification)
BENEFIT: Simpler UX - users can pay immediately without linking wallet first

issues.name: solana-pay-transaction-request
issues.description: Implement Solana Pay Transaction Request spec (server-built transactions via HTTPS endpoint)
steps: CURRENT: We only support Transfer Requests (solana:<recipient>?amount=X) where wallet builds the transaction
TARGET: Also support Transaction Requests (solana:https://api.example.com/pay) where server builds the transaction
Create GET /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint returning { label, icon } per spec
Create POST /v1/solana-pay/:intent_id endpoint accepting { account } and returning { transaction, message? }
Server builds the transaction with the user's account as signer (not fee payer if we want sponsored txs)
Return base64-encoded serialized transaction for wallet to sign and broadcast
Update CreatePaymentIntentQR to optionally return transaction request URL instead of transfer request URL
Add config option to choose between transfer vs transaction request mode
BENEFIT: Enables complex transactions (swaps, multi-instruction), sponsored fees, and server-side transaction building
Write tests for transaction request flow

issues.name: solana-sponsored-transactions
issues.description: Allow merchant to pay Solana transaction fees so users don't need SOL in their wallet
steps: CURRENT: User is fee payer - must have SOL for transaction fees (~0.000005 SOL)
TARGET: Merchant pays fees - users can pay with just USDC/tokens, no SOL needed
Create separate 'fee payer' wallet (distinct from recipient wallet to limit blast radius)
Add fee_payer_wallet and fee_payer_private_key to Solana config (or use KMS reference)
Integrate with AWS KMS or GCP Cloud HSM for secure private key storage (never store raw key in config)
Update BuildPaymentTransaction to set merchant as fee payer (account[0])
Server signs transaction first with fee payer key, then returns partially-signed tx to user
User signs as token sender, transaction now has both signatures
Add rate limiting specifically for sponsored transactions to prevent abuse/draining
Add monitoring/alerting for fee payer wallet balance (alert when low)
Add config flag to enable/disable sponsored transactions (default off)
Consider using Helius or Jito for fee sponsorship as simpler alternative to self-managed keys
TRADEOFF: Adds complexity and security burden (private key on server) but improves UX significantly
Write tests for sponsored transaction flow

issues.name: admin-grants-and-offline-purchases
issues.description: Admin grants products to users (comps, contest winners, manual payments) via dedicated AdminGrant table

design.concept: Admin grants work like user purchases - admin picks a Price/Product, system derives entitlements from Product.EntitlementsSpec

AdminGrant.granted_by: string - admin user ID who made the grant
AdminGrant.payment_id: uuid nullable - only if money was received (links to Payment record)
AdminGrant.duration_days: int nullable - override entitlement duration (null = use Product.EntitlementsSpec)

free_comp: 2. Grant entitlements from Product.EntitlementsSpec (with optional duration override)
manual_payment: 1. Create Payment record with processor='admin', transaction_id (optional external ref)
2. Create AdminGrant record with payment_id pointing to Payment

request.reason: required - arbitrary string explaining the grant
request.amount: optional - if > 0, creates Payment record too
request.transaction_id: optional - external reference (PayPal ID, cash receipt, etc.)

issues.name: credits-balance-system
issues.description: Implement a credits/balance system for consumable purchases (API credits, GPU time, AI tokens)

key_concepts: Each currency = one wallet. Multiple currencies = multiple wallets per user.
Sub-wallets per platform source (Steam, PlayStation, System) - balances sum together
Two balance types: Permanent (never expires) and Time-Limited (expires after set date)
Time-limited balances deplete BEFORE permanent, regardless of source priority
Japan region: 180-day expiration required for paid virtual currency (Payment Services Act)
Credit operations track: amount, source type (Purchase, Promotion, Achievement, Referral_Bonus, Redeem_Code, Other), optional expiration
Full transaction history available for audit
Currency defined as catalog item with FriendlyId (1-3 char code)
Legacy v1 had 'recharge rate' - auto-grant X credits per day (interesting for daily allowances)
Currencies tied to catalog items for purchasing
Less sophisticated than AccelByte - no expiration, no sub-wallets

design_decisions.terminology: Use 'credits' as the generic term. Each credit type is a 'CreditType' (e.g., 'gpu_time', 'api_calls', 'ai_tokens')
design_decisions.expiration: Support optional expiration per credit grant (like AccelByte time-limited balance)
design_decisions.fifo_expiration: Expiring credits consumed first, then permanent credits (AccelByte pattern)
design_decisions.no_sub_wallets: Skip sub-wallets complexity - we don't have multi-platform sources like gaming
design_decisions.ledger_based: Append-only transaction ledger for audit trail, with denormalized balance for fast reads

columns: display_name TEXT NOT NULL -- 'GPU Minutes', 'API Credits'

user_credit_balances.description: Current balance per user per credit type (denormalized for fast reads)
columns: balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 -- total available (permanent + unexpired)
permanent_balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 -- never expires
expiring_balance BIGINT NOT NULL DEFAULT 0 -- sum of unexpired time-limited
earliest_expiry TIMESTAMPTZ -- when next credits expire (for efficient expiry job)

credit_transactions.description: Append-only ledger of all credit changes
columns: amount BIGINT NOT NULL -- positive = deposit, negative = withdrawal
balance_after BIGINT NOT NULL -- balance after this transaction
expires_at TIMESTAMPTZ -- for deposits: when these credits expire (NULL = permanent)

credit_expiry_batches.description: Tracks expiring credit batches for FIFO consumption
columns: INDEX(user_id, credit_type_id, expires_at ASC) -- for FIFO consumption

purchase_flow: RegisterPurchase() calls CreditService.Deposit() for each credit type
Deposit creates transaction + updates balance + optionally creates expiry batch
product_integration.expiration_from_product: Product could specify credits_expiry_days (e.g., 90 days). If set, credits expire 90 days from purchase.

CreditService.Deposit(ctx, userID, creditType, amount,: Add credits, create transaction, update balance
CreditService.Withdraw(ctx, userID, creditType, amount: Deduct credits (FIFO: expiring first), returns ErrInsufficientBalance if not enough
CreditService.ExpireCredits(ctx): Background job to expire old credits and update balances

withdrawal_fifo_logic: 1. Get all unexpired expiry batches for user+creditType, ordered by expires_at ASC
3. If batch depleted, move to next batch
4. After all expiring batches, deduct from permanent balance
6. Create transaction record with amount, balance_after

logic: Find all expiry_batches WHERE expires_at <= NOW() AND remaining_amount > 0
For each: create 'expiry' transaction, set remaining_amount = 0

user_facing: GET /v1/me/credits -- list all credit balances for user
GET /v1/me/credits/:type -- get detailed balance for specific credit type
internal: POST /v1/internal/credits/withdraw -- called by API/GPU service to deduct credits

usage_integration.pattern: External services (API gateway, GPU scheduler) call internal endpoint to withdraw credits before allowing operation
example_flow: User requests GPU job costing 10 credits
GPU service calls POST /v1/internal/credits/withdraw {user_id, credit_type: 'gpu_time', amount: 10, source: 'gpu_job', source_id: job_id}
If success: GPU service proceeds with job
If ErrInsufficientBalance: GPU service rejects with 402 Payment Required

implementation_steps: Create migration for credit_types, user_credit_balances, credit_transactions, credit_expiry_batches tables
Create CreditType model
Create UserCreditBalance model
Create CreditTransaction model
Create CreditExpiryBatch model
Create CreditTypeRepo with CRUD operations
Create CreditBalanceRepo with balance operations
Create CreditTransactionRepo for ledger operations
Create CreditService with Deposit/Withdraw/GetBalance logic
Implement FIFO withdrawal logic with expiry batch consumption
Add CreditsSpec field to Product model
Update RegisterPurchase to grant credits from Product.CreditsSpec
Create River job for credit expiry processing
Create GET /v1/me/credits endpoint
Create GET /v1/me/credits/:type endpoint
Create GET /v1/me/credits/:type/transactions endpoint
Create POST /v1/admin/users/:id/credits endpoint
Create POST /v1/internal/credits/withdraw endpoint
Write tests for deposit/withdraw/expiry flows
Write tests for FIFO consumption order
Document credit system in API docs

issues.name: service-api-endpoints
issues.description: Add X-API-Key authenticated endpoints for server-to-server calls (separate from public/admin)

context.problem: AuthKit and other backend services need to query billing data programmatically without a user JWT
use_cases: AuthKit needs to fetch user entitlements during JWT token issuance (no user JWT available yet)
Backend services may need to check subscription status server-to-server
Future: inter-service communication for credits, usage tracking, etc.
context.solution: Keep a separate private API endpoint (port 8060) with X-API-Key authentication for trusted server-to-server calls

design.authentication: X-API-Key header with shared secret (configured via BILLING_API_KEY env var)
design.endpoint_location: Private port (8060) - NOT exposed to public internet, only internal Docker network

endpoints.description: Get user's active entitlements (for AuthKit token enrichment)
Check if user has active subscription

security_notes: Private port should NEVER be exposed to public internet
API key should be long, random, and rotated periodically
Consider IP allowlisting in production (only allow from known service IPs)
Log all service API calls for audit
Rate limit to prevent abuse even from internal services
implementation_steps: [DONE] Create service API router on private port with X-API-Key middleware
[DONE] Implement GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/entitlements handler
[DONE] Implement GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/subscription-status handler
[DONE] Add APIKey config field and PrivatePort (default 8060) to config.go
[DONE] Add APIKeyRequired middleware with constant-time comparison
[DONE] Update main.go to start both public and private servers
[DONE] Update docker-compose to expose port 8060 only internally (no host binding)
[DONE] Update doujins ServiceClient to use /v1/service routes
[DONE] Update doujins docker-compose with BILLING_ADMIN_URL and BILLING_API_KEY
[DONE] Add GET /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements for admin dashboard use
[N/A] hentai0 doesn't need service API - uses JWT auth for admin endpoints
issues.completed_notes: Implemented private service API on port 8060 with X-API-KEY auth. Added GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/entitlements and GET /v1/service/users/:user_id/subscription-status for server-to-server calls. Also added GET /v1/admin/users/:user_id/entitlements for admin dashboard lookups via JWT auth. doujins ServiceClient updated to use the new /v1/service routes. Docker-compose files updated with BILLING_ADMIN_URL=http://billing:8060 and BILLING_API_KEY env vars.

issues.name: admin-dashboard-metrics-overhaul
issues.description: Overhaul admin dashboard metrics to provide useful business intelligence

metrics_provided: active_users_without_auto_renew - counts active subs where cancelled_at IS NOT NULL (confusing)
active_users_with_auto_renew - counts active subs for CCBill/NMI processors
current_state.processor_metrics: Same 3 metrics broken down by processor

problems: Limited metrics - only subscription counts, no revenue or business KPIs
Confusing naming - 'without_auto_renew' actually means 'cancelled but still in period'
No revenue metrics - MRR, ARR, total revenue, average revenue per user
No churn metrics - churn rate, retention rate, LTV
No Solana support - processor metrics exclude Solana one-time payments
No period comparisons - can't compare this week/month vs previous
No cohort analysis - can't track retention by signup month
No conversion funnel - signups to first payment to recurring
Tests are minimal - just verify response structure, not actual data accuracy

revenue_metrics: MRR (Monthly Recurring Revenue) - sum of active subscription monthly values
Total revenue this period - sum of all payments in date range
Revenue by processor - breakdown by CCBill, NMI, Solana
ARPU (Average Revenue Per User) - total revenue / active users
subscription_metrics: Cancelled subscriptions this period (with breakdown by reason)
churn_metrics: Monthly churn rate - cancelled / active at start of month
Retention rate by cohort - % of month-N signups still active

dashboard_summary.returns: Key KPIs for dashboard cards (MRR, active subs, churn rate, etc.)

churn_analysis.returns: Churn breakdown by reason, cohort retention

implementation_notes: MRR calculation: sum(price.amount / (price.billing_cycle_days / 30)) for active subs
Need to handle different billing cycles (monthly, quarterly, annual)
Solana one-time payments are not subscriptions - track separately
Consider caching expensive aggregations (refresh every 5 min)
Use PostgreSQL window functions for period-over-period comparisons
May want ClickHouse for historical analytics at scale
steps: Define exact metrics needed for admin dashboard UI
Design API response formats for each endpoint
Implement MRR/ARR calculation logic
Implement churn rate calculation
Add revenue aggregation queries
Add one-time purchase tracking (Solana)
Add period comparison support (vs last period)
Add caching layer for expensive queries
Update existing metrics endpoints or create new ones
Write comprehensive tests with seeded data
Document all metrics definitions

issues.name: fix-nmi-add-subscription-missing-payment-info
issues.description: NMI handleAddSubscription() doesn't pass payment info to CreateMembership(), so no Payment record is created for initial subscriptions

context.problem: handleAddSubscription() calls CreateMembership() without TransactionID, Amount, or Currency - meaning new NMI subscriptions don't create Payment records
context.comparison: CCBill handleNewSaleSuccess() correctly passes all payment info to CreateMembership()
context.impact: Initial NMI subscription payments are not recorded in the payments table, breaking revenue tracking

steps: [DONE] Extract amount from body.Plan.Amount in handleAddSubscription()
[DONE] Use price.Currency or default to USD
[DONE] Use 'sub:' + subscriptionID as transaction reference (NMI doesn't include txn ID in subscription add events)
[DONE] Pass TransactionID, Amount, Currency to CreateMembership() in handleAddSubscription()
[DONE] Also fixed handleTransactionSaleSuccess() for pending subscriptions - was missing payment info there too
[NOTE] Tests already exist via webhook replay tests
issues.completed_notes: Fixed both handleAddSubscription() and handleTransactionSaleSuccess() to pass payment info to CreateMembership(). For subscription add events, amount comes from plan.amount (converted from dollars to cents), currency from price or default USD, and transaction ref uses 'sub:' prefix since NMI doesn't include transaction ID in subscription add webhooks. For transaction sale success with pending subscriptions, the actual transaction ID and amount are available and now passed through.

issues.name: add-nmi-refund-void-handlers
issues.description: Add NMI webhook handlers for refund and void events - NMI supports these but we don't handle them

context.nmi_supports: NMI webhooks support 'voids, refunds, and credits' per their docs
context.ccbill_has: CCBill has handleRefund() and handleVoid() with subscription termination logic
context.current_state: NMI only handles transaction.sale.success/failure, recurring.*, acu.*, and chargeback.batch.complete

event_types_to_add: transaction.refund.success - Create refund payment record, optionally terminate subscription if >80% refunded

issues.name: add-nmi-chargeback-subscription-termination
issues.description: NMI chargeback handler only logs batch events - should terminate subscriptions and revoke entitlements like CCBill does

context.current_behavior: handleChargebackComplete() only logs batch-level chargeback data to ClickHouse
context.ccbill_behavior: handleChargeback() terminates subscription immediately and revokes all entitlements
context.problem: NMI chargebacks don't trigger any subscription state changes
context.nmi_limitation: NMI chargeback webhooks are batch-based and do NOT include transaction_id or subscription info, making automatic subscription termination impossible without additional API lookups

steps: [DONE] Parse individual chargeback records from NMI batch webhook
[DONE] Add NMI chargeback batch types (NMIChargebackBatchEventBody, NMIChargebackEntry, etc.)
[DONE] Log individual chargeback events to ClickHouse with requires_manual_review flag
[N/A] Automatic subscription termination NOT POSSIBLE - NMI doesn't provide subscription linkage in chargeback data (unlike CCBill)
[NOTE] Manual review required to match chargebacks to subscriptions via cc_last4, customer_name, and amount
issues.completed_notes: NMI chargeback handler now parses batch data and logs individual chargebacks to ClickHouse. Automatic subscription termination is not possible because NMI doesn't include transaction_id or subscription_id in chargeback webhooks (unlike CCBill). Each chargeback is logged with requires_manual_review=true flag.

ccbill_logs: billing_date_changed - when billing date is modified

CreateMembership, RenewMembership, FailMembership, and CancelMembership now log to ClickHouse via EventLogService. NMI and CCBill webhook handlers still log webhook-specific data separately (complementary, not duplicate).

issues.name: simplify-solana-payment-flow
issues.description: Simplified Solana payment flow - removed wallet linking/verification, payment intents, replaced with direct Solana Pay Transfer Request

context.problem: The Solana payment system was over-engineered with wallet verification challenges, payment intents, and complex state machines
context.solution: Simplified to 3 endpoints: list tokens, create payment URL, check status. No wallet linking required.

changes_made: Removed all wallet/payment-intent endpoints from routes
Kept only: GET /v1/solana/tokens, POST /v1/solana/pay, GET /v1/solana/pay/status, GET /v1/solana/pay/:reference
Payment state stored in Redis with 15-minute TTL (no database table needed)
solana_transactions table retained to link payments to on-chain transactions
new_flow: 1. POST /v1/solana/pay {price_id, token} → returns solana: URL + reference key
2. User scans QR/clicks URL, wallet creates and signs transaction
4. Backend verifies transaction on-chain, creates payment + entitlements
5. Status returns 'completed' with payment_id and signature
issues.completed_notes: All 9 Solana tests pass. Flow is now much simpler - no wallet linking, no verification challenges, no payment intents. Just create payment URL → poll status → done.

issues.name: fix-broken-integration-tests
issues.description: Multiple integration tests are failing due to outdated assertions, wrong field names, and test isolation issues

wrong_field_names: Tests check for 'total_items' but API returns 'total'
Tests check for 'page' and 'page_size' but API uses 'offset' and 'limit'
Tests use 'page=1&page_size=1' query params but API expects 'offset=0&limit=1'
product_seeding: TestGetProductsEndpoint expects 2 products but SeedProducts may return more due to test isolation issues
Multiple test suites seed products independently causing count mismatches
test_isolation: TestTierGroupDetection panics with 'Fail in goroutine after TestAdminGrantsRequireAuth has completed'
Tests share database state and may interfere with each other
Cleanup functions (defer) may run after test context is gone
nmi_config: TestNMIRuntimeClientConfigured checks for real NMI endpoint but test uses mock URL

affected_tests: TestGetNotificationsEmpty - checks total_items instead of total
TestGetNotifications - panics on nil total_items assertion
TestGetNotifications/supports_pagination - checks page/page_size instead of offset/limit
TestListPaymentMethodsEmpty - checks total_items and page instead of total
TestListPaymentMethods - panics on nil total_items assertion
TestTierGroupDetection - goroutine panic due to test isolation
fixes_applied: [DONE] Changed page/page_size → offset/limit in notifications_test.go pagination test
[PARTIAL] Fixed some assertions to use safe type assertions (total, _ := response['total'].(float64))
remaining_fixes: Fix TestGetProductsEndpoint to not hardcode expected count or improve test isolation
Fix TestTierGroupDetection goroutine panic - likely need better cleanup or test ordering
Fix TestNMIRuntimeClientConfigured to accept mock URL or skip when not configured
Review all tests for other total_items/page/page_size references
Consider using test transactions with rollback for better isolation

issues.name: test-architecture-improvements
issues.description: Tests should use real structs and functions from production code, not invent test-specific abstractions

philosophy.principle: Tests should verify actual production code behavior, not test-specific wrappers
philosophy.goal: When production code changes, tests should break if they're testing the affected behavior
philosophy.anti_pattern: Test helper structs that diverge from production models hide API changes and create maintenance burden

current_problems: SubscriptionOptions helper struct uses different field names than models.Subscription (PeriodStart vs CurrentPeriodStartsAt)
Tests can pass while using wrong field names because helpers translate between them
When a model field is renamed/removed, tests may not break because helper abstracts it away
Developers get confused about which struct to use and what fields are available
recommendations: Use models.Subscription directly in tests instead of SubscriptionOptions
Use api.ListResponse[T] for parsing API responses instead of map[string]interface{}
Import and use the actual request/response structs from handlers
If a helper is needed, it should take the real model struct as input, not a parallel struct
Test assertions should use strongly-typed response parsing

example_good.benefit: Uses real model, compiler catches field name errors

refactoring_steps: Audit all test helper structs (SubscriptionOptions, PaymentMethodOptions, etc.)
Replace helper structs with direct model usage where possible
Use json.Unmarshal with actual API response types instead of map[string]interface{}
Add linter rule or CI check to discourage map[string]interface{} in tests
